description_long: This object is a modern military fighter jet with a sleek, aerodynamic fuselage and sharply swept-back wings. The nose is pointed and houses a tinted, bubble-shaped cockpit canopy, likely designed for a single pilot. The wings are mid-mounted and feature underwing pylons for external stores or weaponry, while the tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The jet is equipped with a single engine, visible at the rear, and has a tricycle landing gear configuration with the main gear under the wings and a nose gear. The surface is finished in a combination of dark and light gray tones, with some markings and insignia visible on the tail and fuselage. The overall design emphasizes speed, maneuverability, and combat readiness.
description: A modern military fighter jet with swept-back mid-mounted wings, a single vertical tail, and a bubble canopy cockpit. The jet is finished in gray tones with visible underwing pylons and a single rear engine.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pointed nose, bubble canopy, and the symmetrical sweep of the wings with underwing pylons visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the elongated fuselage, cockpit placement, swept-back wings, and detailed tail markings.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the single engine exhaust, vertical stabilizer, and the horizontal stabilizers extending from the tail.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the cockpit, wing configuration, and tail details with visible landing gear doors.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with sharply swept-back wings and underwing pylons; no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a bubble canopy
